en: An empty little room. A girl is sitting on the floor, crying.
Why are you crying?
My cat turned into a mushroom.
How could a cat possibly turn into a mushroom?
She looks up at you with melancholy eyes. I don't want to talk about it.
Who did this?
A magician. His face is cunning and hideous. His wand is cold and sharp. He is the mother of devils. He is the son of gamblers.
I will help you find the magician and make him turn the cat back.
Thank you for your help. But when you find him, please just kill him. I don't need him anymore.
Is it a poisonous mushroom or a regular one? This might provide some useful clues.
My cat had a glossy, smooth coat and a gentle, docile temperament. As a mushroom, it is brilliantly colorful and dazzling, making one's mouth water.
I understand. I will help you get your cat back.
You have found the magician. He stands in a dark forest, his wand glowing with a cold light.
Hello, magician.
Hello, unkempt girl. Your hair is too long. Please allow me to improve it for you.
I met a heartbroken child. Her cat turned into a mushroom. Do you know anything about this?

Cats are evil creatures. They are born unruly and rebellious, yet they seduce others with their thick fur and beautiful appearance. Mushrooms are kind creatures. They can be chopped into small pieces and put into pizza, burgers, soup, fried rice, buns, and dumplings.

I hate eating mushrooms. I hate mushrooms in my pizza. I hate mushrooms in my burgers. I hate mushrooms in my soup. I hate mushrooms in my fried rice. I hate mushrooms in my buns. I hate mushrooms in my dumplings. Mushrooms can be chopped so small they're barely visible, but their scent seeps into every single grain of rice.

I love eating mushrooms. Mushrooms are wonderfully chewy and delicious. I love mushroom pizza, mushroom burgers, mushroom rice, mushroom buns, mushroom dumplings, and mushroom soup. The greatest food in the century is mushrooms.

I am, after all, a reasonable man. Since your girl is saddened by her cat becoming a mushroom, it means our intentions differ. Different paths lead to different destinations. I respect everyone's opinion, but I do not believe what I did was wrong.
You are an unrepentant sorcerer. I will make you realize your mistake.
Battle begins!
The magician has fallen. Behind him, you discover the mushroom—brilliantly colorful, dazzling and vivid, faintly glowing in the darkness.
Is this the mushroom that was once a cat...
Hello, mushroom. Even as a mushroom, you are still one of the more beautiful ones. I have never seen such a vibrant mushroom. Such a perfectly round mushroom.

Everyone who has tasted me has died. I feel the passage of time, the cruelty of nature. I walk alone through an endless dark night, with only the rain to cleanse me. The moon warms at midnight's third watch. At high noon, a crescent moon hangs. And I am merely the most ordinary among thousands upon thousands of mushrooms. Why do you speak to me?
I met a girl even more melancholic than you. She said she misses your cat form. I cannot bear to see others shed tears. So I journeyed far, killed the magician, and came to bring you back.

I was once a cat. The magician turned me into a poisonous mushroom, letting me drink deeply of nature's rain and dew. As a cat, merely existing in stillness could be interpreted as seduction. But becoming a mushroom has filled my life with laughter.
In the quiet depths of night, heaven and earth weep for me. On the windy nights, the thoughts of darkness drift away. Ah, what shall become of me now!

Then there is no need to deceive yourself any longer. The mushroom's laughter is a lie you tell yourself. The cat's silence is the only moment you were truly honest. The magician is dead. His spell is buried in the earth with him. But I still remember your true form—
The spell takes effect. The mushroom's cap slowly contracts and curls, and soft fur and whiskers grow anew...
Dakara boku wa kinoko wo yameta.
Come with me!
Hello, mushroom. Even as a mushroom, you are still one of the more beautiful ones. I have never seen such a vibrant mushroom. Such a perfectly round mushroom.

I favor the dark, damp corners. This is my garden. I need not meow, need not groom, need not stretch—and yet I can enjoy endless, boundless tranquility. Nature is the inexhaustible treasure of creation, and I am merely a devoted mushroom.

Every mushroom has its own mastery. I could become a cat and make the girl scream for me. I could become a cockroach and make the girl scream for me. I could become a mushroom and make the girl weep for me. Admittedly, to be adored is something. But only as a mushroom can one enjoy the silent rainforest. I merely exist in stillness, watching all these passing emotions, while I remain forever unchanged.
It seems you have fully embraced your identity as a mushroom.
I was born a mushroom. A mushroom is a mushroom. That is the natural order of things. Truth, love, the world, suffering, life—whatever, it's all fine.
Then I shall say no more. To disturb a desireless, peaceful tranquility is in itself an offense. May you continue being a mushroom, and live happily ever after.
You turn and walk away. Behind you, the mushroom glows quietly in the dark.
